# convolve.convolve { #fmrimod.convolve.convolve }

```python
convolve.convolve(x, hrf=None, sampling_rate=1.0, sampling_frame=None, **kwargs)
```

Convolve event(s) with hemodynamic response function.

This is a generic function that convolves various event types with an HRF.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name           | Type                              | Description                                                                                                                | Default    |
|----------------|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| x              | [object](`object`)                | Event object or array to convolve                                                                                          | _required_ |
| hrf            | HRF, array, or None               | Hemodynamic response function. If None, uses SPM canonical HRF                                                             | `None`     |
| sampling_rate  | [float](`float`)                  | Sampling rate in Hz (default 1.0). Ignored if sampling_frame is provided.                                                  | `1.0`      |
| sampling_frame | [array](`array`) - [like](`like`) | Explicit time points at which to sample the convolved signal. If provided, takes precedence over sampling_rate.            | `None`     |
| normalize      | [bool](`bool`)                    | If True, peak-normalize each regressor column after convolution so that max(abs(col)) == 1. Default False.                 | _required_ |
| summate        | [bool](`bool`)                    | Whether overlapping HRF responses are summed (default True). If False, max is used instead. Passed to fmrimod.regressor(). | _required_ |
| **kwargs       |                                   | Additional arguments passed to convolution                                                                                 | `{}`       |

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type                           | Description      |
|--------|--------------------------------|------------------|
|        | [Array](`fmrimod.types.Array`) | Convolved signal |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> # Convolve event with default HRF
>>> event = EventVariable(onsets=[1, 5, 10], durations=[2, 2, 2],
...                      values=[1, 2, 3], name="stimulus")
>>> convolved = convolve(event, sampling_rate=0.5)  # TR=2s
```

```python
>>> # Use custom HRF
>>> from fmrimod import HRF
>>> custom_hrf = HRF(a1=6, a2=12)
>>> convolved = convolve(event, hrf=custom_hrf)
```

```python
>>> # Use explicit sampling frame
>>> sampling_times = np.arange(0, 100, 2.0)  # TR=2s
>>> convolved = convolve(event, hrf=custom_hrf, sampling_frame=sampling_times)
```
